title: Sweet Creamy Ethiopian Peanut Butter Stew
description: A comforting, sweet and creamy stew that combines nutty peanut butter with fresh vegetables, a touch of rice vinegar, and aromatic Ethiopian spices, perfect for mealprep and Whole30friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger; sauté until the onion becomes translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced carrot, diced sweet potato, and diced red bell pepper to the pot; stir to combine.
Sprinkle in the cinnamon, cardamom, and cayenne pepper; cook for 1 minute to toast the spices.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and stir in the unsweetened coconut milk, allowing the stew to bubble gently for 10 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Take a small portion of the hot stew liquid and whisk it into the smooth peanut butter until fully emulsified and smooth.
Gradually pour the peanut butter mixture back into the pot, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Simmer the stew for an additional 5-7 minutes, letting the flavors meld and the sauce thicken slightly.
Stir in the rice vinegar and season with salt to taste.
Serve hot, or let cool completely and refrigerate for meal‑prep. Enjoy over cauliflower rice or as a hearty soup.
Serve immediately for best flavor and texture.
